backend/pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Maven property defining the target Java version from 17 to 21.
backend/pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the build configuration claims to target Java 17, which is inconsistent with the JDK 21 migration goal and may cause confusion or incorrect tooling behavior if other plugins rely on this property for source/target levels.

backend/pom.xml.general_idea: Explicitly set the maven-compiler-plugin release flag to 21 to ensure bytecode compatibility with JDK 21.
backend/pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the compiler might default to an older source/target level (e.g., 17 or 8), resulting in bytecode that does not utilize JDK 21 features or may fail to compile if JDK 21-specific APIs are used without the correct release flag.

backend/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade maven-compiler-plugin to version 3.11.0 to ensure support for JDK 21 compilation.
backend/pom.xml.why_exists: Older versions of maven-compiler-plugin may not fully support JDK 21 features or may fail to compile code targeting Java 21 due to lack of support for the 'release' flag or newer language constructs.

backend/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Lombok to version 1.18.30 to ensure compatibility with JDK 21.
backend/pom.xml.why_exists: Older versions of Lombok may not support JDK 21, leading to compilation errors or runtime failures due to incompatibilities with the new JDK's internal APIs or module system.
